ayahs.text: وَالذّٰرِيٰتِ ذَرْوًا ۝١ۙ

explanations.explanation: In the word 'ذَرْوًا', at the 3rd letter (position 3 of 4), we find Noon Tanween. The next letter 'ف' (from the word 'فَالْحٰمِلٰتِ') is not from the throat letters, not from Yarmaloon, not Ba, and not Alif. Therefore, we apply Ikhfa (إخفاء), which means you must hide or conceal the Noon with a nasal sound (ghunnah). The sound is between clear pronunciation and complete merging - not fully pronounced, not fully merged.
explanations.urdu_explanation: لفظ 'ذَرْوًا' میں، تیسرا حرف ، نون تنوین ہے۔ اگلا حرف 'ف' (لفظ 'فَالْحٰمِلٰتِ' سے) نہ حلقی حروف میں سے ہے، نہ یرملون، نہ با، اور نہ الف۔ یہاں اخفاء ہوگا۔ نون کو غنہ کے ساتھ چھپا کر بولیں۔

ayahs.text: اِنَّمَا تُوْعَدُوْنَ لَصَادِقٌ ۝٥ۙ

This means we apply Idgham (إدغام), which is the rule of merging. Since the letter 'ن' is one of (ي و م ن), you must perform idgham with a nasal sound (ghunnah), also called Idgham with Ghunnah (إدغام بغنة).
